generated_requirements.text: The system shall be implemented as a Windows-based Multi-Document Interface (MDI) client application deployable on a standard client PC within the exercise operations center.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 and the In-Scope section mandate a Windows-based MDI client with no server-side or cloud dependency for the core display function.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically retrieve and refresh all Event and Activity data from Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 on a fixed 60-second cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 and Business Objective 1 require automated 60-second data refresh to ensure real-time situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to manually pause the automatic 60-second data refresh cycle and resume it on demand.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 and the In-Scope section require pause and resume capability to support briefing moments or display freeze requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 as read-only data sources using their existing data interfaces, without modifying either source application.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and BR-06 require read-only integration with both source systems via existing interfaces, with no edits performed within EEMDS.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display only MSEL Summary data in both the graphical timeline and the data table regions; full MSEL detail records shall not be displayed.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 restricts the data scope to MSEL Summary data only, keeping full detail records out of scope.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allocate the left two-thirds of the display area to a graphical timeline view and the right one-third to a data table region; this layout ratio shall be fixed and not user-configurable.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 mandates a fixed two-thirds/one-third screen split between the graphical and tabular regions.

generated_requirements.text: The graphical timeline region shall plot Events and Activities on a timeline graph, visually representing their scheduled and actual positions relative to current exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Future State description requires a graphical timeline that enables instant visual triage of schedule adherence.

generated_requirements.text: The data table region shall be vertically segmented such that the top one-quarter displays sequential (order-dependent, non-time-bound) Events and the bottom three-quarters displays Events ordered by temporal proximity to current exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 defines the mandatory segmentation and ordering rules for the data table region.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall color-code all displayed Events and Activities based on their variance from current exercise time, conforming to DHS color schema thresholds (e.g., on-time, at-risk, overdue) defined during requirements elaboration.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 and Business Objective 2 require color-coded schedule variance indicators to enable rapid visual triage.

generated_requirements.text: All visual design elements of the application — including backgrounds, text, highlights, and status indicators — shall conform to the Department of Homeland Security color schema; no custom or ad hoc color choices are permitted without formal DHS approval.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and RC-01 mandate strict adherence to the DHS color schema across all UI elements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display two independent, simultaneously visible clocks at all times: one showing current local (wall-clock) time and one showing current exercise time, each visually distinct from the other.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 and the Future State description require dual-clock display to eliminate time confusion during exercises where exercise time diverges from wall-clock time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a direct deep-link from each displayed Event or Activity to its corresponding record in the originating source application (Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0), enabling users to navigate to the source record for modification without leaving the display context.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 and the In-Scope section require deep-link navigation back to source applications since EEMDS is a display tool, not an editing tool.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to filter displayed Events and Activities by Venue, independently or in combination with other active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 mandates filtering by Venue as one of the two required filter dimensions to reduce cognitive load during execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to filter displayed Events and Activities by Key Events designation, independently or in combination with other active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 mandates filtering by Key Events designation as the second required filter dimension.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an unfiltered view of all Events in the exercise, accessible on demand regardless of any currently active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 requires that a full, unfiltered exercise view always be accessible to prevent loss of situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to define and configure user-defined time segments used to organize and display Events on the timeline, configurable per exercise prior to or during exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 and the In-Scope section require user-configurable time segments to support exercise-specific timeline organization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and maintain a history report that captures all changes to Event or Activity data (as reflected from source systems) throughout the exercise, available for retrieval at any point during or after the exercise.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 and Business Objective 6 require a complete audit history to support post-exercise after-action review.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide actual versus assumed time variance charts for Events and Activities, enabling quantitative analysis of schedule adherence.
generated_requirements.rationale: The In-Scope section and Future State description require actual vs. assumed time charts for variance analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate each user at login and deny access to any user who is not pre-authorized by the system administrator; no guest or anonymous access modes shall be supported.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 and Business Objective 7 require positive authentication and denial of unauthorized access with 100% enforcement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control, ensuring that only users with appropriate roles can access specific display and configuration functions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Future State description and RC-02 require role-based access control aligned with DHS IT security policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support integration with an externally managed user identity source (e.g., directory service or administrator-managed list) for user authorization; EEMDS shall not perform user provisioning or identity management.
generated_requirements.rationale: A-04 and the Out-of-Scope section establish that user identity management is external to EEMDS.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render the display layout and all text at font sizes and proportions sufficient to be legible at a viewing distance of up to 100 feet on a 10×10 projection screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 2 and TC-04 require projection-optimized readability at up to 100 feet for shared situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: Event and Activity detail data shall be readable by 90% of viewers at a viewing distance of 30 feet on a 10×10 projection screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 8 sets a measurable readability criterion of 90% of viewers at 30 feet for detail-level data.

generated_requirements.text: Color-coded Event status indicators shall be interpretable by 90% of viewers at a viewing distance of 100 feet on a 10×10 projection screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 2 sets a measurable success criterion for color-coded status comprehension at maximum projection distance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 99% of the time during normal business hours (0800–1800 local time, Monday through Friday) over the first six months of operation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 3 and OC-01 mandate 99% availability during normal business hours as a key success criterion.

generated_requirements.text: Planned maintenance windows shall be scheduled exclusively outside of normal business hours (outside 0800–1800 local time, Monday through Friday).
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-01 requires that maintenance not impact availability during normal business hours.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete each 60-second data refresh cycle without perceptible degradation in display performance or usability.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-02 requires that the refresh cycle not introduce visible lag or usability issues during live exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: Data staleness shall never exceed 60 seconds under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 1 sets a maximum data staleness of 60 seconds as a measurable KPI for real-time situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: 90% of new users shall be able to independently initiate an Event or Activity display within 90 minutes of first use, without formal training.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 4 sets a measurable learnability criterion to minimize the learning curve for exercise controllers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with applicable DHS IT security policies governing user authentication and access control for internal operational tools.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-02 mandates compliance with DHS IT security policies for authentication and access control.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall correctly distinguish authorized from unauthorized users in 100% of access attempts, resulting in zero unauthorized access incidents.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 7 sets a zero-tolerance success criterion for unauthorized access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall consolidate and display all Events and Activities from both Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0 in a single unified display without requiring manual data entry by users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 5 requires full consolidation of both source systems into a single display to eliminate fragmented data access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the current exercise time sourced from the source applications or an exercise clock service accessible to EEMDS.
generated_requirements.rationale: A-07 establishes that exercise time is maintained by source applications or an exercise clock service and must be consumed by EEMDS.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall classify Events as sequential or temporal based on a field or attribute already present in the MSEL data from source applications, without performing its own classification logic.
generated_requirements.rationale: A-08 specifies that the sequential vs. temporal distinction is governed by an existing MSEL data attribute, not EEMDS logic.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to configure and modify display settings prior to or during exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: The In-Scope section includes display configuration and modification capabilities as a required feature.

generated_requirements.text: The history report shall record, at minimum, the identity of the changed record, the nature of the change, and the timestamp at which the change was reflected in EEMDS.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 and Business Objective 6 require a complete, retrievable history to support after-action reconstruction of the exercise timeline.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render correctly on projection equipment capable of displaying a 10×10 screen image connected to the client PC, with no additional configuration required at projection time.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-04 and OC-03 require that the display render correctly on standard projection equipment used in operations center environments.

generated_requirements.text: The MDI application framework shall support simultaneous display of both the graphical timeline and the data table within a single application window.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-03 mandates that the MDI framework support concurrent rendering of both display regions in one window.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not provide any capability to directly create, edit, or delete Events or Activities within EEMDS; all data modifications shall occur exclusively in the originating source application.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 and the Out-of-Scope section explicitly prohibit data editing within EEMDS to preserve source system integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not integrate with any data source other than Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Out-of-Scope section restricts integration to the two designated source applications only.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not provide automated alerting or notification mechanisms outside the visual display (e.g., email or SMS alerts).
generated_requirements.rationale: The Out-of-Scope section explicitly excludes automated alerting outside the visual display.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall gracefully handle unavailability of either source application (Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0) by displaying a clear status indicator to the user without crashing or corrupting the display.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-01 and D-02 identify source system unavailability as a dependency risk that the system must handle gracefully.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all user authentication attempts, including both successful and failed login events, with timestamps and user identifiers.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-02 and BR-08 require enforcement of access control policies, which necessitates an authentication audit trail.

generated_requirements.text: The system codebase and configuration shall be structured to allow the IT Administrator to deploy, update, and maintain the application on client PCs without requiring source code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 and the IT Administrator stakeholder role require that the system be maintainable and deployable by operations staff.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the Exercise Planner role with the ability to define and save time segment configurations on a per-exercise basis before exercise execution begins.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-06 and BR-10 require that time segments be configured by exercise planners prior to execution for the display to function meaningfully.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the After-Action Review (AAR) Analyst role to retrieve the history report of all Event and Activity data changes at the conclusion of each exercise.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 6 and the AAR Analyst stakeholder need require accessible post-exercise history reporting.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall visually distinguish the local time clock from the exercise time clock using clearly differentiated labels and/or visual styling so that users cannot confuse the two.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 requires the two clocks to be visually distinct to prevent confusion in exercises where exercise time diverges from wall-clock time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain the history report data persistently so that it remains available for retrieval after the exercise concludes, even if the application is restarted.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 requires the history report to be available at any point during or after the exercise, implying persistent storage.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall apply combined filters (Venue and Key Events designation simultaneously) and update the display in real time without requiring a manual refresh.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 allows filters to be applied in combination, and real-time filter response is necessary to support fast-moving exercise scenarios.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed and documented such that a developer familiar with the technology stack can understand, modify, and extend any module within a reasonable timeframe, with inline code documentation and a maintained technical design document.
generated_requirements.rationale: Long-term operational sustainability requires that the system be maintainable by the development and IT teams supporting DHS exercise operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to search MLS property listings by specifying criteria such as location, price range, property type, and other relevant attributes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core field productivity feature enabling realtors to query MLS data without returning to the office.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display MLS property search results including clear property images and relevant listing details.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agents need visual and descriptive property information to advise clients on-site.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return MLS property search results within 30 seconds of query submission under normal operating conditions, 100% of the time.
generated_requirements.rationale: A hard performance ceiling defined in the BRD to ensure agents can respond to clients without delay.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to create new property listings in the MLS from their mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enables agents to submit new listings from the field, eliminating the need to return to the office.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to update existing MLS property listings from their mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agents must be able to keep listing data current while working in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce data validation rules on all property listing creation and update operations before submitting data to the MLS, preventing malformed or incorrect data from being introduced.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 mandates MLS data integrity through validation controls on all write operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to generate a Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) report based on property criteria explicitly specified by the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: CMA generation is a core productivity feature that currently requires office access and significant manual effort.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not generate a CMA report without explicit property criteria input from the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 requires that CMA reports are always criteria-driven to ensure relevance and accuracy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and deliver a CMA report within 60 seconds of the realtor submitting the required criteria.
generated_requirements.rationale: A hard performance ceiling defined in the BRD to enable on-the-spot client pricing advice.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a map showing property locations for a realtor-specified area, using data from the MLS dataset accessible to the authenticated realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integrated mapping eliminates the need for separate consumer tools and reduces agent workflow friction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide turn-by-turn driving directions to a selected property from within the application, restricted to properties that exist within the authenticated realtor's accessible MLS dataset.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 and the future-state vision require integrated directions to eliminate reliance on separate navigation tools.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to download, view, and manage client contacts synchronized from the office system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agents need current contact data in the field to eliminate reliance on paper notes and memory.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to download, view, and manage appointments synchronized from the office system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Field access to appointment data is essential for eliminating scheduling inefficiencies identified in the problem statement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically synchronize client contacts and appointment data with the office system once every hour without requiring any action from the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 mandates hourly automatic synchronization to ensure field data is never more than 60 minutes stale.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor automatically when new appointments are discovered during the hourly synchronization with the office system.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 requires proactive notification so agents are immediately aware of new appointments without manual checking.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall initiate calls to sellers or buyers to schedule appointments on behalf of the realtor, using only contact phone numbers associated with that realtor's client records.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 and the future-state vision require automated appointment scheduling to reduce coordination lag.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically notify the realtor when a seller or buyer responds to an appointment request initiated by the system, without requiring the realtor to manually check for updates.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 mandates response-driven notifications to eliminate scheduling lag and improve appointment responsiveness.

generated_requirements.text: All appointment-scheduling communications initiated by the system must be traceable to the realtor's account.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires traceability of system-initiated communications for accountability and compliance purposes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate in a read-only offline mode when internet connectivity is unavailable, allowing realtors to access previously downloaded search results, CMA reports, and appointments.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 and the future-state vision require offline capability so agents are productive regardless of connectivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall queue write operations that cannot be completed while offline and automatically submit them to the appropriate backend systems upon reconnection.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 requires that offline write operations are not lost but deferred until connectivity is restored.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to save property search results for later review, and saved results must remain accessible in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 requires search result retention and offline accessibility to support field workflows without connectivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall save user preferences — including search filters, display settings, and saved results — and restore 100% of those preferences to their last known state following any system failure or connectivity interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 mandates complete preference persistence to protect data integrity across sessions and failure events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate every user at the start of each session and authorize access before permitting use of any system function.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 and the security success criterion require that 100% of users are authenticated and authorized before any access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deny access to any individual who is not formally registered as a realtor with the organization, at all system entry points.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 mandates that unregistered individuals are blocked from accessing proprietary MLS and client data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve zero unauthorized access incidents by enforcing authentication and authorization controls on every access attempt.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD security success criterion explicitly requires zero unauthorized access incidents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be installable by an untrained realtor on their smart device without the use of printed instructions, and at least 90% of untrained realtors must be able to complete installation successfully.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD success criterion and operational constraint require self-service installation to eliminate onboarding overhead.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be usable by realtors with no formal training on the product, presenting a self-explanatory user interface using terminology natural to the real estate profession.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD requires that 90% of untrained realtors can use the system immediately, eliminating training overhead.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 24 hours per day, 365 days per year, achieving a minimum availability rate of 99.9% (no more than 1 failure per 1,000 access attempts).
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD availability success criterion requires 24/7/365 uptime to support agents operating at all hours.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 1,000 simultaneous users at launch without degradation of performance or availability.
generated_requirements.rationale: The current registered realtor population of 1,000 defines the minimum concurrent user capacity required at go-live.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall be scalable to support 5,000 simultaneous users within 12 months of launch without requiring a system redesign.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD projects a fivefold growth in the realtor population within 12 months, requiring planned scalability.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall run natively on devices operating the Windows CE operating system.
generated_requirements.rationale: The technical constraints section specifies Windows CE as one of the two mandatory target platforms.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall run natively on devices operating the Palm operating system.
generated_requirements.rationale: The technical constraints section specifies Palm OS as one of the two mandatory target platforms.

generated_requirements.text: The user interface shall conform to the native UI standards and conventions of the target smart device platform, including standard menus and navigation buttons.
generated_requirements.rationale: The technical constraints require platform-native UI conformance to ensure intuitive use on each target device.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restore 100% of saved user preferences to their last known state upon recovery from any system failure or connectivity interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and the data integrity success criterion require complete preference restoration after any failure event.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store user preferences and cached data locally on the smart device to support offline operation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Assumption A-09 and BR-04 require local storage of preferences and cached content to enable offline mode.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the MLS programmatic interface (API) to retrieve property listings, display property images, and submit new or updated listings.
generated_requirements.rationale: Assumption A-03 and the MLS scope items require a programmatic integration with the external MLS data provider.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the office system's synchronization interface (API or data feed) to perform hourly synchronization of contacts and appointments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Assumption A-02 and BR-03 require a stable integration with the office-side system for data synchronization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with a licensed third-party mapping data provider to supply property location maps and driving directions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Assumption A-06 and BR-09 require a third-party mapping service to power the integrated navigation feature.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall utilize the smart device's native telephony capability to initiate calls to sellers or buyers for appointment scheduling.
generated_requirements.rationale: Assumption A-07 and BR-10 require use of the device's native calling capability for appointment communication.

generated_requirements.text: All system-initiated communications with buyers and sellers must comply with applicable telecommunications and consumer contact regulations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.4 identifies regulatory compliance for consumer communications as a mandatory constraint.

generated_requirements.text: Access to MLS data must be restricted to authenticated realtors who hold valid MLS membership and authorization, in compliance with real estate industry regulations and MLS membership rules.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.4 identifies MLS access governance as a regulatory and industry constraint that the system must enforce.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect the loss of internet connectivity and automatically switch to offline (read-only) mode, transparently informing the realtor of the degraded state.
generated_requirements.rationale: Agents must be aware of their connectivity state so they understand which operations are available to them.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically resume full online operation and process any queued write operations when internet connectivity is restored.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 requires that deferred write operations are submitted upon reconnection to maintain data consistency.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a reliable synchronization mechanism such that contacts and appointments are never more than 60 minutes stale under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD success criterion for data currency requires a maximum data staleness of 60 minutes.

generated_requirements.text: The system's backend infrastructure shall be provisioned and maintained to support between 1,000 and 5,000 concurrent users, as managed by the IT team prior to system go-live.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dependency D-05 requires that IT provisions scalable backend infrastructure before launch to meet capacity requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall reference the organization's realtor registry during authentication to verify that a user is formally registered before granting access.
generated_requirements.rationale: Assumption A-04 and BR-01 require the authentication system to validate users against a current realtor registry.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present the installation process in a manner that guides an untrained realtor to successful completion without requiring separately printed instructions or IT assistance.
generated_requirements.rationale: The operational constraint and success criterion require a fully self-guided installation experience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that driving directions and property maps are only available for properties that exist within the MLS dataset accessible to the currently authenticated realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 restricts mapping and directions to MLS-listed properties to enforce data access boundaries.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log and make traceable all appointment-scheduling calls initiated on behalf of a realtor, linking each communication to the realtor's account and the associated client record.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires traceability of system-initiated communications for audit, accountability, and regulatory compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed and documented such that backend components — including the authentication server, synchronization server, and middleware — can be maintained and updated by the IT team without requiring changes to the mobile application.
generated_requirements.rationale: Separating backend maintainability from the mobile client ensures the system can scale and evolve without full redeployment.

ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to create, modify, and delete Programs of Study, each defined by a unique program name and a required set of associated classes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Centralizing Program of Study management is a core capability identified in Section 6.1 and Business Rule 5.1.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to create and modify student cohorts, where each cohort is uniquely identified by its start month and year and is associated with exactly one Program of Study.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.1 mandates that every cohort be tied to exactly one Program of Study and be uniquely identified by start month and year.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to define and modify a preferred class sequence — including the quarter in which each class will be offered — for each cohort.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.1 requires that a preferred sequence with quarter assignments be definable per cohort to support scheduling operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce that only students who are eligible for a cohort's Program of Study may be added to that cohort.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.1 explicitly prohibits adding ineligible students to a cohort, requiring the system to validate eligibility at enrollment time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators and Nursing Staff Members to add and modify nursing student records, including personal information, enrollment status (full-time or part-time), program enrollment, and cohort assignment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Student record management is listed as an in-scope capability in Section 6.1 and is required by both stakeholder groups.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall classify each nursing student as either full-time or part-time, and shall allow staff members to modify a part-time student's class sequence and timeline independently of the standard cohort sequence.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rules 5.4 and Section 6.1 require support for part-time student deviations from the standard cohort sequence.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to add and modify classes in the Nursing Department's catalog, classifying each class as either clinical or non-clinical, with no class permitted to be both or neither.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.2 mandates a strict binary classification for all classes, and class catalog management is in scope per Section 6.1.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require that each non-clinical class record specifies, at minimum, the course name, lecture room requirements, and instructor needs.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.2 defines the minimum required attributes for non-clinical class records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require that each clinical class record specifies, at minimum, the course name, lecture room requirements, clinical site needs, lecture instructor needs, and clinical lab instructor needs.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.2 defines the minimum required attributes for clinical class records, which are more complex than non-clinical classes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect scheduling conflicts between classes assigned to the same cohort and shall prevent any conflicting schedule from being saved.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.2 and the Problem Statement identify automated conflict detection as a non-negotiable requirement to eliminate a primary source of scheduling errors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to create, modify, and cancel clinical lab sections, where each section is associated with an existing clinical class and specifies the clinical site name, assigned instructor, and day and time of the lab.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.3 and Section 6.1 define clinical lab section management as a core in-scope capability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent the cancellation of a clinical lab section if any students are currently enrolled in that section, displaying an appropriate error message to the user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.3 explicitly prohibits cancellation of a lab section that has enrolled students.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow staff to enroll a student in a clinical lab section only if that student is already registered for the corresponding clinical class.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.3 requires that clinical lab section enrollment be contingent on the student's registration in the parent clinical class.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow staff to move a student from one clinical lab section to another, provided both sections correspond to the same clinical class.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.3 explicitly permits inter-section transfers within the same clinical class as a supported operation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a centralized repository of clinical site records, each including the site name, contact person name, and contact information, accessible only to authorized users.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 and the Problem Statement identify the lack of a centralized clinical site record as a key operational gap to be resolved.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to view all clinical lab sections scheduled at a given clinical site for any specified quarter.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 4.2 identifies the ability to view all labs at a site per quarter as a required future-state capability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically send email notifications to all affected parties when any of the following events occur: a student's schedule is published or updated; a clinical section's capacity changes or is cancelled; a class is cancelled or its details change; a cohort's sequence or weekly schedule is modified; a student's status changes; or a student is moved to a different clinical section.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.6 enumerates all triggering events for automated notifications, which is a primary mechanism for eliminating manual ad hoc communications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use stored email addresses as the sole primary channel for all outbound automated communications.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.6 designates stored email addresses as the primary communication channel for all system-generated notifications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a contact information repository storing email addresses and phone numbers for all relevant parties, including staff, students, lecture instructors, clinical lab instructors, and clinical site administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 lists a contact information repository as an in-scope capability required to support automated notifications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate on-demand cross-cohort quarterly planning reports that list all classes needed for a specified quarter, including sections, labs, and room types, without requiring manual data aggregation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 5 and Section 6.1 require on-demand planning reports to eliminate the current manual aggregation process.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate printable summary reports for individual cohorts, clinical sites, clinical lab sections, and individual students.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 explicitly lists printable summaries for these entities as in-scope reporting capabilities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a system log recording significant system events and shall provide a printable summary of the system log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 includes a system log with printable summary capability as an in-scope feature for auditability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall interface with CampusConnect's central server to support the final class section scheduling function, accessible only to Dr. Susan Poslusny and Karen Sysol.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 and Business Rule 5.5 require a CampusConnect integration restricted to specifically named authorized users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall interface with the faculty central server to synchronize faculty data, reducing manual re-entry of instructor information.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 and Section 4.2 identify integration with the faculty central server as a required future-state capability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall interface with the student master server to access student eligibility and enrollment data, reducing manual re-entry and improving data consistency.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 and Section 4.2 identify integration with the student master server as a required future-state capability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access controls such that clinical site details (including contact person and contact information) are accessible only to Program Administrators and Nursing Staff Members.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.5 explicitly restricts clinical site details to Program Administrators and Nursing Staff Members only.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access controls such that student personal information is accessible only to Dr. Susan Poslusny and Dr. Julie Donalek, and is inaccessible to all other users including other Program Administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.5 names specific individuals authorized to access student personal information, requiring technically enforced restrictions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict access to the CampusConnect interface function exclusively to Dr. Susan Poslusny and Karen Sysol.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.5 designates only two named individuals as authorized to use the CampusConnect interface.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform data integrity validation on all data entry operations to prevent incorrect or invalid data from being stored.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.7 mandates basic data integrity validation on all data entry as a non-negotiable rule.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall trigger an automated notification to all affected parties when a student's status changes (e.g., from full-time to part-time).
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rules 5.4 and 5.6 both require automated notification upon student status changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete general student management tasks (adding students, updating records, viewing summaries) within 5 seconds under normal load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 3 and Section 7.2 define a 5-second response time KPI for general student management tasks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete schedule generation operations within 30 seconds under normal load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 3 and Section 7.2 define a 30-second response time KPI specifically for schedule generation operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 10 simultaneous users between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM without degradation in response time performance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 3 and Section 7.1 specify a minimum concurrent user load of 10 during peak business hours.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum uptime of 95% measured during the operational window of 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM on business days.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 3 and Section 7.2 define a 95% uptime KPI within the defined operational window.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall technically enforce role-based access controls to ensure zero unauthorized access incidents to protected data categories, including student personal information, clinical site details, and the CampusConnect interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 4 sets a KPI of zero unauthorized access incidents, requiring technically enforced rather than policy-only access controls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall protect student personal information in accordance with DePaul University's information security and data privacy policy and applicable federal student privacy regulations (e.g., FERPA).
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.3 mandates compliance with institutional data privacy policy and implies FERPA compliance for student educational records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use DePaul University's standard organization-approved virus protection software for threat monitoring, with no alternative security tooling substituted.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.1 explicitly requires use of the organization's standard virus protection software as a non-negotiable technical constraint.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be operable within the Windows XP Professional operating environment used on computers in the Nursing Department's business offices.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.1 constrains the system to the Windows XP Professional environment currently in use by the department.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be accessible via any HTML-compatible browser without requiring proprietary browser plugins.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.1 requires HTML browser compatibility to ensure accessibility across the department's existing browser landscape.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be learnable by Program Administrators and Nursing Staff Members such that at least 2 out of every 3 users can independently complete core scheduling and student management tasks after one day of training.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 1 and Section 7.4 define a measurable learnability KPI tied to a single day of training.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall be sufficiently intuitive that Program Administrators and Nursing Staff Members can navigate core workflows without consulting documentation during normal operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.5 requires an intuitive UI to support unaided operation by non-technical users during day-to-day tasks.

generated_requirements.text: All terminology used within the system shall be consistent with Standard English and the recognized vocabulary of the national Nursing Community; non-standard domain jargon shall not be introduced.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.7 and Section 7.5 mandate terminology alignment with nursing community standards to ensure usability for domain experts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be architected to remain viable and capable of managing nursing program operations for a minimum of five years from deployment, and shall be capable of storing and managing student and class data for a ten-year horizon.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 2 and Section 7.4 require long-term viability of at least five years with a ten-year data horizon.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable and operable within the standard business office environment of the DePaul University Nursing Department using existing hardware and network infrastructure, without requiring infrastructure upgrades.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.4 constrains deployment to existing hardware and network infrastructure to avoid additional capital expenditure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall conform to the documented interface specifications of CampusConnect's central server, the faculty central server, and the student master server when implementing integrations with those platforms.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 7.1 requires conformance to existing platform interface specifications to ensure integration compatibility and long-term maintainability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be capable of managing 100% of nursing students and their associated classes across all active cohorts for a rolling ten-year planning horizon.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 2 defines a KPI requiring the system to handle the full student population and class catalog over a ten-year horizon.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deliver all automated email notifications to affected parties in a timely manner following a triggering event, eliminating the need for manual ad hoc outreach for routine scheduling communications.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 2 requires that all routine scheduling communications be delivered via automated email, replacing unreliable manual processes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall reduce student-reported scheduling errors by at least 50% within the first twelve months of go-live compared to the baseline period immediately prior to deployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 1 defines a measurable KPI for error reduction as the primary indicator of system reliability and correctness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall serve as the single authoritative source of truth for cohort management, class scheduling, clinical site assignments, and student progress tracking, eliminating duplicate records and version-control inconsistencies.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 2 and the Problem Statement identify the absence of a single source of truth as a root cause of operational errors that the system must resolve.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict direct student self-service access; nursing students shall interact with the system only as recipients of automated communications and shall not be granted login or data-entry access.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.2 explicitly places direct student self-service access out of scope, requiring the system to enforce this boundary.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via a logon process before granting access to any application function, permitting only users with valid, active accounts to proceed.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 mandates that only users with valid, active accounts may log on, establishing authentication as a core functional gate.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control with at least two access levels — View and Update — restricting dispute case creation and modification exclusively to users with Update access.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 specifies that only Update-access users may initiate or modify disputes, while View-only users are limited to search and view operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict each user's access to dispute cases belonging solely to their authorized cardholder base, technically preventing cross-portfolio access.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 strictly prohibits cross-portfolio access, requiring the system to enforce cardholder-base boundaries for every user.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prohibit external users from requesting original receipts; the Request for Original Receipt action shall be available only to internal users.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 explicitly reserves the original receipt request action for internal users, excluding all external partner users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow revoked user accounts to be reinstated only by a System Administrator; no other user role shall have the capability to reinstate a revoked account.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 designates the System Administrator as the sole authority for account reinstatement to prevent unauthorized privilege restoration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall dynamically filter and present the list of available follow-up actions for a dispute case based on (a) the current status of the case and (b) the access level of the logged-in user.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 requires that follow-up action menus be context-sensitive to both case status and user role to prevent unauthorized or inapplicable actions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present only transactions that fall within the eligible dispute age window — as defined by applicable regulations — when a user initiates a dispute, filtering out ineligible transactions.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 requires programmatic enforcement of transaction age eligibility to ensure regulatory compliance during dispute initiation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the initiation of a single dispute case covering multiple transactions, provided all selected transactions belong to a single merchant.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 explicitly permits multi-transaction dispute cases under the constraint that all transactions share a single merchant.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect and block the creation of a duplicate dispute request; if a dispute already exists for a given transaction, any subsequent dispute initiation attempt on that same transaction shall be rejected with an appropriate user notification.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 mandates 100% prevention of duplicate disputes to avoid erroneous financial adjustments and reconciliation problems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support three types of ticket retrieval requests: (1) Request for Original Receipt, (2) Request for a Copy of the Receipt, and (3) Request for a Portfolio, each with its own eligibility rules enforced at initiation.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 defines the three distinct retrieval request types that the system must support with type-specific eligibility enforcement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support two types of chargeback requests — Pending and Immediate — and shall prompt the user for all required information specific to the selected chargeback type during case initiation.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 requires the system to differentiate between Pending and Immediate chargebacks and collect type-specific data accordingly.

generated_requirements.text: Upon successful creation of a ticket retrieval request, the system shall display a confirmation containing: the dispute case number, the type of retrieval requested, and the date the merchant response is due.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 specifies the exact confirmation data elements required after a successful ticket retrieval request creation.

generated_requirements.text: Upon successful creation of a chargeback request, the system shall display a confirmation containing: the dispute case number, the type of chargeback (Pending or Immediate), and the date the merchant response is due.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 specifies the exact confirmation data elements required after a successful chargeback request creation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall dynamically derive and present the list of eligible dispute reason codes based on (a) the type of transaction being disputed and (b) the industry classification of the merchant involved; unconstrained reason code selection shall not be permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-14 prohibits generic reason code selection and requires context-driven filtering to ensure regulatory and operational accuracy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically record every action that creates or modifies a dispute case — whether initiated by a user or an automated system process — in the case history, capturing the user's name or system identifier, the date of the action, and a description of the action taken.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-15 mandates a comprehensive, tamper-evident audit trail for every dispute case to support internal auditing and regulatory examination.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record all additions of new users and all modifications to existing users' access levels in a User Report that is available for audit review.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-16 requires a dedicated User Report to capture access control changes, supporting the organization's audit and compliance obligations.

generated_requirements.text: When an automated (non-user-initiated) process acts on a dispute case, the system shall notify the user who originally initiated that dispute case upon their next logon.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-17 ensures originating representatives are made aware of systematic case actions so they can take timely follow-up action.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically purge dispute cases that have been closed for more than 6 months from the online disputes database via a systematic, automated purge process.

generated_requirements.text: All business rules implemented in the Disputes System shall comply with Regulation E (Electronic Fund Transfer Act) and Regulation Z (Truth in Lending Act) as they govern credit card dispute processing.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-19 and C-01 establish regulatory compliance with Regulation E and Regulation Z as a non-negotiable functional requirement.

generated_requirements.text: All business rules implemented in the Disputes System shall comply with the Merchant Operating Regulations governing chargeback procedures and retrieval request handling.

generated_requirements.text: All letter requests generated by the Disputes System shall be formatted in accordance with the specifications required by the Print Letter Utility and submitted exclusively through that application; the Disputes application shall not generate or print letters independently.

generated_requirements.text: All financial adjustment requests to cardmember or merchant accounts shall be submitted exclusively through the Cardmember Posting and Billing System; the Disputes application shall not post adjustments directly to any account.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dispute case search capability allowing users to search by dispute case number, merchant account number, cardmember account number, and issuer number, with filtering by date range, dispute type, case status, and reason code.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD scope section defines these specific search and filter parameters as required capabilities for case lookup.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display dispute case search results with color-coded status indicators, including at minimum yellow for open cases and gray for closed cases, enabling representatives to instantly identify case status.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD future state and scope sections specify a color-coded results interface to provide real-time case status visibility.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate in real time with the Statement Database to retrieve transaction and statement data, sourcing all such data exclusively from that system without maintaining its own copy of transaction records.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-05 prohibits the Disputes application from maintaining its own transaction records, requiring real-time integration with the Statement Database.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate in real time with the Cardmember Information Database to retrieve cardmember account data, sourcing all such data exclusively from that system without maintaining its own copy of cardmember master data.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-04 prohibits the Disputes application from maintaining its own cardmember master data, requiring real-time integration with the Cardmember Information Database.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate in real time with the Merchant Information Database to retrieve merchant data, sourcing all such data exclusively from that system without maintaining its own copy of merchant master data.

generated_requirements.text: The System Administrator shall be able to add new user accounts, assign and modify access levels (View or Update), revoke accounts, and reinstate revoked accounts through the Disputes application.

generated_requirements.text: The System Administrator shall be able to generate a User Report listing all user account additions and access level modifications, including the acting administrator, date, and change detail, for audit review.

generated_requirements.text: End users shall not have direct access to any underlying data files or databases; all data interaction shall occur exclusively through the Disputes application's user interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-09 explicitly prohibits direct database or data file access by end users to enforce data integrity and security.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be accessible by both internal users (employees) and external partner users simultaneously, with access restrictions appropriate to each group enforced at the system level.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support screen-to-screen navigation response times not exceeding 5 seconds under normal and peak load conditions within the supported concurrency range.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 350 concurrent users without measurable performance degradation in response times or functional behavior.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-10 and the success criteria mandate a minimum concurrency of 350 users to support the organization's multi-user operational environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year, with the only permitted downtime being scheduled maintenance windows: Monday through Saturday, 3:00 AM to 4:00 AM EST, and Sunday, 1:00 AM to 5:00 AM EST.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-12 and the availability success criteria define the precise uptime and maintenance window schedule required to support continuous dispute operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce authentication and role-based access controls such that 100% of unauthorized access attempts — including attempts to access cases outside a user's cardholder base or to perform actions beyond their access level — are blocked.
generated_requirements.rationale: The security objective and BR-01 through BR-05 collectively require complete enforcement of access boundaries with zero unauthorized access.

generated_requirements.text: The system's audit trail records shall be tamper-evident; no user — including System Administrators — shall be able to alter or delete case history entries or User Report entries once recorded.
generated_requirements.rationale: The audit trail objective requires tamper-evidence to ensure legal and regulatory defensibility of dispute case histories.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be sufficiently intuitive that 100% of cardmember and merchant services representatives can perform their core job functions — including dispute initiation and follow-up actions — immediately following a 2-day training course, with no additional on-the-job support required for first attempts.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-14 and the rapid adoption success criteria place a stringent usability constraint on all user-facing workflows to achieve 100% adoption post-training.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall comply with the organization's corporate standards for user interface creation, applicable to both internally and externally accessed application screens.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-03 mandates adherence to corporate UI standards, prohibiting non-standard frameworks without explicit approval.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide guided, step-by-step workflows for dispute case initiation — including ticket retrieval requests and chargeback notifications — with real-time prompts for all required information at each step.
generated_requirements.rationale: The future state and adoption success criteria require guided workflows to minimize errors and enable first-attempt success after training.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain reliable, automated duplicate detection such that zero duplicate dispute requests are created on the same transaction across all operational conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The centralization objective's success criteria require 100% duplicate prevention reliability to protect financial reconciliation integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The automated data purge process for dispute cases closed more than 6 months shall execute reliably on a scheduled basis without manual intervention, ensuring consistent database hygiene.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-18 requires the purge to be systematic and automated, implying reliable scheduled execution without human dependency.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed and documented to allow business rules — particularly those governing regulatory compliance, reason codes, and dispute eligibility — to be updated without requiring full system redevelopment, supporting ongoing regulatory change.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ongoing compliance with evolving regulations (Regulation E, Regulation Z, Merchant Operating Regulations) requires that business rules be maintainable independently of core system logic.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed to support integration with the Statement Database, Cardmember Information Database, Merchant Information Database, Print Letter Utility, and Cardmember Posting and Billing System via documented, stable interfaces that can be maintained or updated independently of the Disputes application core.
generated_requirements.rationale: The integration architecture must be maintainable to accommodate future changes in upstream or downstream systems without requiring full redevelopment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record the complete case history — including all user-initiated and system-initiated actions with actor identity, date, and action description — for 100% of dispute cases, with no gaps or omissions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The audit trail objective's success criteria require complete and uninterrupted case history recording to support legal and regulatory examination.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce that all dispute case initiations and follow-up actions are performed exclusively through the Disputes application's user interface; no direct database or data file access by end users shall be technically possible.
generated_requirements.rationale: The centralization objective and C-09 require that the application be the sole point of interaction, eliminating bypass risks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall programmatically enforce all applicable business rules — including transaction eligibility, reason code derivation, duplicate detection, and access restrictions — on every transaction processed, achieving 100% compliance with Regulation E, Regulation Z, and Merchant Operating Regulations.
generated_requirements.rationale: The regulatory compliance objective requires that compliance be achieved through programmatic enforcement rather than individual representative knowledge.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deliver logon notifications of systematic case actions to the originating representative reliably upon their very next logon session following the automated action, with no missed notifications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain response time performance at or below 5 seconds per screen navigation when operating at maximum supported concurrency of 350 simultaneous users, with no degradation relative to single-user performance.
generated_requirements.rationale: The performance success criteria explicitly require that the 5-second response time ceiling hold under peak load, not just average conditions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a web-based recycled parts search function accessible exclusively to users with the Collision Estimator role.
