generated_requirements.text: The recycled parts search shall accept vehicle information and damaged parts information as search inputs and query the ChoiceParts data feed for available recycled parts.
generated_requirements.rationale: The future state requires estimators to search by vehicle and parts information against the ChoiceParts integration.

generated_requirements.text: The recycled parts search shall first query the estimator's list of preferred parts suppliers before expanding the search scope.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 mandates that the primary search is conducted against preferred suppliers to maximize cost savings.

generated_requirements.text: If no recycled parts are found from preferred suppliers, the system shall automatically expand the search to all suppliers within a 30-mile radius of the vehicle's ZIP code and notify the estimator of the fallback.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 requires an automatic geographic fallback to ensure maximum parts availability is surfaced to the estimator.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent an estimator from applying recycled parts to a collision estimate when the search returns no available parts.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 requires this guard to maintain data integrity and compliance with estimatics rules.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall persist the recycled parts search results associated with each estimate so they can be retrieved during a subsequent audit.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 and BR-14 require that original search results are retained to enable accurate audit comparison.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a preferred repair facility search function accessible to users with the Adjuster role, accepting vehicle year, make, model, street address, city, state, ZIP code, and a search radius between 1 and 30 miles as inputs.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 defines the required inputs for the preferred repair facility search to enable data-driven facility selection.

generated_requirements.text: When the vehicle ZIP code is not provided, the system shall automatically resolve it from the street address, city, and state using an address resolution service.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires ZIP code resolution to ensure the facility search can proceed even when the ZIP is unknown.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return the repair facility with the highest ratings matching the search criteria as the recommended preferred repair facility.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 requires the system to surface the top-rated facility to standardize and improve repair quality outcomes.

generated_requirements.text: If no rated repair facility is found within the specified radius, the system shall return a list of all repair facilities within that radius for the adjuster to select from manually.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 ensures the adjuster always has actionable options even when no rated facility exists in the area.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow an adjuster to override the system-recommended preferred repair facility and assign a different facility to the estimate.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 grants adjusters the authority to override recommendations to accommodate legitimate business exceptions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users with the Adjuster role to request a recycled parts audit report for a specified collision estimate.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 restricts audit report requests to adjusters to enforce appropriate role-based governance.

generated_requirements.text: The audit report shall display: (a) total number of recycled parts used in the estimate, (b) total number of available recycled parts from the original search, (c) percentage of available recycled parts that were used, and (d) a total audit score summing individual line item scores.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-15 specifies the exact content of the audit report to support compliance measurement and supervisory review.

generated_requirements.text: Audit reports shall be accessible to any adjuster at any later point in time, not restricted to the adjuster who originally requested the report.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-16 requires shared audit report access to support collaborative review and regulatory examination.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow users with the Supervisor Adjuster role to view, enter, and save ratings for repair facilities on a scale of 1 to 10 across defined rating categories.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 and BR-17 restrict rating modification to supervisor adjusters and define the 1–10 scale.

generated_requirements.text: Repair facility rating categories shall include, at minimum, 'attempted use of recycled parts' and 'actual use of recycled parts'.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-18 mandates these specific categories to align ratings with recycled parts compliance objectives.

generated_requirements.text: When no prior ratings exist for a repair facility, the system shall display a blank ratings entry form rather than an error message.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-19 ensures a graceful user experience for new facilities that have not yet been rated.

generated_requirements.text: Standard adjusters and collision estimators shall be able to view repair facility ratings but shall not be able to create or modify them.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 enforces read-only access to ratings for non-supervisor roles to maintain data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control with three distinct roles — Collision Estimator, Adjuster, and Supervisor Adjuster — granting each role access only to the functions defined for that role.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 through BR-03 collectively require strict role-based access control across all system functions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce company-level data isolation such that users of one insurance company cannot view, access, or modify claim data belonging to another insurance company.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 mandates strict multi-tenant data isolation to prevent legal and regulatory exposure in shared-services deployments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronize recycled parts data from the ChoiceParts data feed once per day at approximately 1:00 AM.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-22 and TC-11 fix the integration window to ensure data currency while respecting the external provider's schedule.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all user-entered data against defined business rules and data constraints before persisting any record, displaying clear validation error messages for invalid inputs.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-21 requires comprehensive input validation to prevent corrupt or invalid data from entering the system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide in-system contextual help on at least 90% of application screens.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table and scope section require 90% help screen coverage to support learnability and reduce support burden.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain an audit trail of all claims-related data changes sufficient to support Sarbanes-Oxley financial controls and regulatory examination.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-12 and TC-13 require auditability of claims data to satisfy SOX and estimatics law compliance obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be accessible via Internet Explorer version 5.5 and above and Mozilla Firefox version 1.0 and above.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and the scope section mandate support for these specific browser versions to match the corporate and repair facility environment.

generated_requirements.text: Recycled parts search results shall be returned to the user within 15 seconds under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table specifies a 15-second response time target for recycled parts search to meet estimator productivity goals.

generated_requirements.text: Preferred repair facility search results shall be returned to the user within 8 seconds under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table specifies an 8-second response time target for facility search to support adjuster workflow efficiency.

generated_requirements.text: Audit reports shall be generated and returned to the requesting user within 10 seconds of the request being submitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table specifies a 10-second audit report generation target to ensure timely supervisory review.

generated_requirements.text: Repair facility rating save operations shall complete and confirm to the user within 5 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table specifies a 5-second save time target for ratings to maintain supervisor adjuster productivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 1,500 simultaneous users at launch without degradation of defined response time targets.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table and TC-07 require the system to handle 1,500 concurrent users at go-live.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall be designed to scale to support 10,000 concurrent users within two years of launch without requiring a fundamental redesign.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-07 and the Year 2 KPI require scalability planning to be built into the initial architecture.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be capable of processing approximately 50,000 recycled parts records per day during the nightly ChoiceParts data synchronization.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table specifies a daily data volume of 50,000 records that the system must handle without failure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve 98% uptime during all scheduled availability windows, with unplanned downtime not exceeding 2% of scheduled available time.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-08 and the KPI table define a 98% uptime SLA to ensure the system is reliably available to field users.

generated_requirements.text: All system maintenance activities shall be scheduled and executed within established corporate maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-09 requires maintenance to be confined to approved windows to protect scheduled availability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce authentication and authorization controls to ensure that only authenticated users with appropriate roles can access system functions and data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based access control and multi-tenant isolation require robust authentication and authorization as a security foundation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be free of computer viruses and shall include protections against malicious attacks, including denial-of-service attacks.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-15 explicitly mandates virus-free software and DoS protections to safeguard claims data and system availability.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmissions between the client browser and the server shall be encrypted using industry-standard protocols to protect sensitive claims and personal data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Insurance regulatory compliance and SOX requirements necessitate encryption of data in transit to prevent unauthorized interception.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with applicable insurance regulations governing claims processing in all jurisdictions where it is deployed.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-14 mandates compliance with insurance regulations to avoid regulatory penalties and legal exposure.

generated_requirements.text: 95% of adjusters and collision estimators shall rate the system as easy to use in post-go-live surveys.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table sets a 95% ease-of-use target as a primary success criterion for user adoption.

generated_requirements.text: A new user shall achieve full proficiency with the system within two days of onsite training.
generated_requirements.rationale: The KPI table defines a two-day learnability target to minimize training investment and accelerate time-to-productivity.

generated_requirements.text: The user interface shall be designed to be operable in physically demanding repair facility environments, including dirty and noisy conditions with limited controlled workspace.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-06 requires the UI to accommodate the harsh physical environment in which collision estimators work.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to corporate UI guidelines and corporate color scheme standards as defined by the Corporate Architecture & UI Standards Team.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-04 mandates adherence to corporate UI and color standards to ensure brand consistency and governance compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall adhere to corporate architecture guidelines governing technology stack selection, integration patterns, and deployment architecture.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-03 requires alignment with corporate architecture standards to ensure long-term supportability and integration compatibility.

generated_requirements.text: All product installations and upgrades shall be performed exclusively by the middleware technology team within established corporate launch timeframes.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-05 restricts deployment authority to the middleware team to maintain governance and reduce deployment risk.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be supportable through the existing corporate support center without requiring a dedicated support team.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-10 and assumption A-02 require the system to fit within existing support processes to avoid additional operational cost.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate entirely on existing corporate hardware infrastructure across development, test, and production environments without requiring new hardware procurement.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 prohibits new hardware procurement, requiring the system to be deployable on current infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall handle failures in the ChoiceParts data feed gracefully, displaying an appropriate message to users and continuing to operate with the most recently synchronized data.
generated_requirements.rationale: The ChoiceParts dependency is identified as a critical risk; graceful degradation protects the estimator workflow during feed outages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear, user-friendly error message when the ZIP code resolution service is unavailable, and shall allow the adjuster to manually enter a ZIP code to continue the repair facility search.
generated_requirements.rationale: The dependency table identifies ZIP code resolution as a risk; a fallback mechanism prevents complete workflow failure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with the Sarbanes-Oxley Act requirements, particularly financial controls and auditability of all claims-related data creation, modification, and deletion events.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-12 mandates SOX compliance, requiring a complete and tamper-evident audit trail for all financial and claims data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with estimatics laws governing the use of recycled parts in collision repair estimates, ensuring that the audit trail and report outputs are sufficient to support regulatory examination.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-13 requires compliance with estimatics regulations, making the audit trail a legal and regulatory necessity.
